summary: Tested TYS Fase 0 POC end-to-end. 18/19 backend tests pass, all frontend critical flows work. Found 1 CRITICAL backend bug in Stripe status polling endpoint (502 due to emergentintegrations library issue). Frontend handles the error gracefully but the polling endpoint itself is broken.

critical.issue: Returns 502 instead of 200 when polling a freshly created session. Two distinct failure modes observed in emergentintegrations library: (1) Pydantic validation error 'metadata Input should be a valid dictionary [type=dict_type, input_value=<StripeObject ...>]' for CheckoutStatusResponse.metadata field; (2) 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' from Stripe when querying a session that was just created via emergentintegrations.create_checkout_session. This BREAKS the Success page polling flow (the entire (a)/(b)/(c) validation path required by the user spec). Frontend handles the 502 gracefully without crashing, but the polling never succeeds.

minor.issue: When called without valid Stripe-Signature, the underlying emergentintegrations.handle_webhook throws KeyError: 'type' before signature check; server still returns 400 (correct) but logs noisy stack trace. Consider catching this earlier.

integration_issues.issue: Polling endpoint returns 502 so the page always shows 'Hubo un error' with Stripe error text. UX is graceful (no crash, shows error + polling counter + 'Ver pagos' & 'Volver al inicio' buttons) but the happy path is unreachable due to the backend bug above.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix /api/poc/stripe/status/{session_id} 502 errors. Root cause is in emergentintegrations library: CheckoutStatusResponse.metadata Pydantic model expects dict but receives stripe.StripeObject; also 'No such checkout.session' errors suggest the wrapper may be querying the wrong Stripe environment/account. Suggested fixes: (a) wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in a try/except and on validation/Stripe errors, fallback to returning DB-only status (db_status='pending', payment_status='unknown', amount_total=0) with HTTP 200 so the frontend polling can still iterate without 502; (b) consider calling Stripe SDK directly instead of via emergentintegrations for the status retrieval; (c) escalate to Emergent integrations team about the StripeObject->dict serialization bug.
Minor: webhook handler — pre-validate that body parses as JSON with an 'type' key before delegating to handle_webhook, so invalid bodies don't generate misleading KeyError logs.
critical_code_review_comments: Good: All endpoints under /api prefix, openapi_url='/api/openapi.json' set. MongoDB _id properly excluded via projection {'_id': 0}. Seed is idempotent (upsert+$setOnInsert with unique index on slug). Pydantic validation working for amount_cents>0 and Literal plan_name.
Good: Frontend uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exclusively, data-testid attributes present on all required elements.
Issue: server.py mixes @app.on_event (deprecated in FastAPI) — consider migrating to lifespan context manager.
Issue: Nav data-testid uses spanish-with-accent 'nav-suscripción' which is unusual for testids (works but typically testids avoid non-ASCII). Consider renaming to 'nav-suscripcion'.

Issue: poc_stripe_status raises 502 on ANY Stripe failure; this prevents the frontend from displaying the persisted DB state. Consider returning 200 with payment_status='error' and the DB status when Stripe call fails.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7 critical flows incl. Stripe redirect verified to checkout.stripe.com)

test_credentials: No auth in Fase 0. Stripe test card 4242 4242 4242 4242 not actually used since backend status endpoint failed before reaching Stripe UI.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Existing seed (demo-org, prueba-eventos) verified idempotent — exactly 2 tenant docs after multiple backend restarts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covering health, tenant resolve, Stripe session creation, payment listing, status polling, webhook, seed. After main agent fixes the /api/poc/stripe/status/{session_id} 502 issue (likely by wrapping the stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status call in try/except and falling back to DB-only status), re-run pytest and verify TestStripeStatus::test_status_for_fresh_session passes with HTTP 200 and db_status='pending'. Frontend Success page should then progress through polling instead of immediately showing error. The /api/openapi.json correctly serves under /api (root /openapi.json hits SPA fallback which is expected ingress behavior).
rca of the issue: RCA for /api/poc/stripe/status/{session_id} 502: The endpoint calls emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status(session_id). The library internally retrieves the Stripe Checkout Session via stripe-python SDK and parses the result into a Pydantic CheckoutStatusResponse model. When Stripe returns the session with metadata, stripe-python wraps metadata as a stripe.stripe_object.StripeObject (dict-like but not dict instance), which fails Pydantic v2 validation `dict_type`. Reproduction: POST /api/poc/stripe/create-subscription-session returns cs_test_XXX, then immediately GET /api/poc/stripe/status/cs_test_XXX -> 502 with detail containing 'metadata Input should be a valid dictionary'. A second failure mode: 'No such checkout.session' from Stripe API itself, suggesting the wrapper (sk_test_emergent) may route create/retrieve via different paths or there is a propagation delay/account mismatch on Emergent's Stripe wrapper. Mitigation in TYS server.py: wrap get_checkout_status call so that on ANY exception we return 200 with the DB status and stripe_payment_status='unknown' so the frontend polling loop can keep functioning until the webhook arrives (which is the documented fallback path).
